abawd_enforced_2026_detail: ABAWD/RTW is actively enforced statewide in FY2026. Arkansas has NO approved geographic ABAWD time-limit waiver on the USDA FNS FY2025-2029 waiver list (states with FY2025 waiver responses are AK, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DE, DC, KY, IL, ME, MI, NV, NH, NJ, NM, NY, OR, RI, SD, VI, WA, WI — Arkansas is absent). The AR SNAP Manual states Arkansas has not been under a waiver since RTW resumed 01/01/2016. OBBBA/HR1 (signed July 2025) further (a) expanded the ABAWD age range from 18-54 to 18-64, and (b) restricted geographic waivers to areas with unemployment over 10%, removing the prior 'lack of sufficient jobs' basis. Arkansas additionally transitioned SNAP E&T from voluntary to MANDATORY for non-working ABAWDs effective April 2026. A secondary source claimed '15 rural counties still have exemptions'; this is unconfirmed by the official FNS waiver list and appears to conflate ABAWD discretionary (15%) exemptions or labor-market-area data with geographic waivers — no county geographic waiver for AR is documented in primary sources.
general_volunteer_lane_detail: Two distinct routes exist in AR policy. (Route 1 — General RTW unpaid/volunteer lane, 80 hrs/mo): SNAP Manual Section 3500 (RTW, markup dated 03/01/2026) states an ABAWD must 'Work at least twenty (20) hours per week (or an average of eighty (80) hours a month). Work can be for pay, for goods or services... unpaid, or as a volunteer; ... This can be verified with the Volunteer Agreement Form (DCO-0261).' Section 3540.3 repeats: 'Worked at least eighty (80) hours per month ... (this can be verified with the Volunteer Agreement (DCO-0261)).' This lane counts general nonprofit volunteering at the flat 80-hour federal standard. (Route 2 — Comparable Workfare, reduced formula): Section 3700/3730 allows fulfilling RTW via Comparable Workfare, where required hours = monthly SNAP allotment / minimum wage (fewer hours), but ONLY when the site is an authorized 'Comparable Workfare entity' that meets reporting/supervision requirements and the eligibility worker issues the DCO-261 within that framework.
qualifying_org_definition: Churches, local government agencies (any federal, state, county, city, or town), and other non-profits willing to supervise the work and meet reporting requirements to establish participation. Per Section 3700, 'Comparable Workfare providers are limited to churches, local government agencies, such as any federal, state, county, city, or town, and other non-profits who are willing to meet the reporting requirements to establish the individual's participation and supervision.' The same entity types serve as the supervising organization that completes the DCO-261 Volunteer Agreement for the general 80-hour volunteer lane.
verification_instrument: DCO-261 Volunteer Agreement (in the current Section 3000 manual markup written 'DCO-0261'). Completed by the supervising entity (church, local government, or nonprofit) and used by the county eligibility worker to verify volunteer hours toward RTW. Appendix A describes it as 'Used by counties to verify volunteer hours for individuals volunteering to meet work requirements.' Per Section 3751.2, 'Issue the DCO-261 Volunteer Agreement to be completed by the Comparable Workfare entity/individual who will supervise any volunteer activity' — i.e., the same form serves both the general 80-hour volunteer lane (Sections 3500/3540.3) and the Comparable Workfare reduced-formula track (Section 3730).
required_prestep_detail: For the general 80-hour volunteer lane (Section 3500/3540.3), no EDP, county assignment, or provider MOU is required before hours count — the household notifies the county and the supervising org's DCO-0261 verifies hours. For the REDUCED-FORMULA route (Comparable Workfare, Section 3730), the site must function as an authorized 'Comparable Workfare entity' willing to meet reporting/supervision requirements and the eligibility worker issues the DCO-261 (the individual may self-locate a placement, but only with an authorized allowable entity). The reduced (allotment/min-wage) hours are therefore gated on Comparable Workfare provider status; general nonprofit volunteering not run as Comparable Workfare counts at 80 hrs/mo.
remote_stance_detail: No primary source addresses remote/virtual volunteering. Section 3500 lists 'unpaid' and 'volunteer' work without specifying on-site presence; Comparable Workfare 'job sites' and supervision language implies a supervised placement but does not explicitly require physical presence. Treat as silent.
county_variation_detail: Policy is uniform statewide; SNAP is state-administered through the DHS Division of County Operations. Comparable Workfare 'may operate in any county.' Mandatory E&T is referenced as available in 'E&T counties,' but the general 80-hour volunteer lane and Comparable Workfare are statewide. No material per-county variation in the rules themselves.
classification_reasoning: The allotment/minimum-wage formula (Section 3730: 'dividing the household's authorized monthly SNAP benefit amount (before recoupment) by the current state or federal minimum wage, whichever is greater; fractions rounded down') applies ONLY to Formal and Comparable Workfare, i.e., when the volunteer site is an authorized Comparable Workfare entity that meets DHS reporting/supervision requirements and the worker issues the DCO-261. General nonprofit volunteering that a recipient self-initiates counts under the RTW unpaid-work lane (Sections 3500/3540.3) at the flat 80 hrs/month. A recipient cannot obtain the lower formula hours simply by volunteering at any 501(c)(3); the reduced hours require the Comparable Workfare framework. This matches the 'workfare_if_registered' definition exactly.
monthly_hours_at_max_allotment_detail: Under the Comparable/Formal Workfare formula (Section 3730), at the FY2026 maximum 1-person SNAP allotment of $298 (48 states, eff. 10/01/2025) divided by AR minimum wage $11.00, rounded down = 27 hours/month. Most recipients with lower allotments owe fewer. By contrast, the general (non-workfare) volunteer lane is a flat 80 hrs/month. Prior file used $291/$11=26 with an older allotment; FY2026 max is $298 → 27.
hours_formula_detail: General volunteer lane (Section 3500/3540.3): 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week) for unpaid/volunteer work verified by DCO-0261. Comparable/Formal Workfare track (Section 3730): required hours = monthly SNAP allotment / max(state, federal minimum wage), fractions rounded down — applies only when the site is an authorized Comparable Workfare entity. Manual NOTE 2 confirms: 'For E&T Work Experience and Workfare Programs, the household's obligation of work hours required to meet the RTW will be calculated by dividing the household's authorized monthly SNAP benefit amount (before recoupment) by the current state or federal minimum wage, whichever is greater. Fractions are rounded down.' Manual EXAMPLE: '$194 / $8.50 = 22.82 ... needs 23 hours per month to meet the RTW rather than 80.'
minimum_wage_detail: Arkansas state minimum wage is $11.00/hr in 2026 (set by 2018 ballot Issue 5; no COLA indexing, stable into 2026), higher than the federal $7.25, so $11.00 is used in the Section 3730 workfare formula ('state or federal minimum wage, whichever is greater').
regional_wages_detail: Arkansas has a single statewide minimum wage; no sub-state regional minimum wages affect the formula.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Section 3700 limits Comparable Workfare providers to 'churches, local government agencies... and other non-profits' with no geographic restriction stated; policy is silent on whether the nonprofit must be Arkansas-based. Comparable Workfare 'may operate in any county.' No primary source imposes an in-state requirement, but none affirmatively permits an out-of-state 501(c)(3) either. Operationally a supervising org must be able to meet DHS reporting/supervision requirements and complete the DCO-261, which an out-of-state org could in principle do, but this is not confirmed in policy.
geographic_waivers: none — Arkansas has no approved ABAWD geographic time-limit waiver. Absent from the USDA FNS FY2025-2029 ABAWD waiver list. RTW applies statewide and has since 01/01/2016. OBBBA/HR1 (July 2025) restricts future waivers to areas with unemployment >10%, making AR waiver eligibility even less likely.
submission_mechanism_detail: The supervising organization completes the DCO-261/DCO-0261 Volunteer Agreement; it is submitted to / verified by the local DHS county office (the eligibility worker issues and collects it). For ABAWDs who have used countable months, the form may be submitted with the household's Semi-Annual Report (SAR) to re-establish eligibility (see manual Example #1: Sam 'provided the DCO-261 with his SAR'). No online volunteer-hours portal exists for the general lane; the E&T track separately uses provider MIS reporting (SNAP Works), which does not gate the DCO-261 lane.
reporting_cadence_detail: Hours accrue against a monthly 80-hour (or workfare-formula) obligation. The household must notify the county when a member meets RTW (Section 3540.3); the worker determines compliance within 10 days of the reported change. For ABAWDs who have used countable months, re-verification occurs at recertification/SAR. Comparable Workfare sponsors/entities maintain participation records (Section 3752).
submission_deadline: unknown — no fixed calendar deadline (e.g., 'by the 10th') is published. The household must notify the county office when RTW is met (Section 3540.3); the worker then has 10 days to determine compliance. The DCO-261 states the months and start date for which volunteering will count, and re-verification is tied to the recertification / Semi-Annual Report cycle. Add to unresolved.
form_required_fields_detail: DCO-261 fields are inferred from policy description (the fillable form is not publicly posted as a PDF). The manual confirms the form records the supervising entity and start date ('Comparable Workfare participation will begin with the entity and the date shown on the DCO-261'), the months the person may volunteer, and the hours needed to meet RTW (Example #1). Notarization is not required.
conflicts_with_prior: CLASSIFICATION CHANGED: prior file = 'federal_80' (hours_formula=federal_80, tier=1_clean); verified = 'workfare_if_registered'. The prior file correctly identified the 80-hour general unpaid-work lane (Section 3500/3540.3, DCO-0261) but treated it as the whole picture and labeled the manual's allotment/min-wage workfare formula as merely a 'separate track.' Primary-source reading (Section 3730 + NOTE 2 + Section 3751.2) shows the reduced allotment/min-wage hours ARE available, but ONLY through Comparable/Formal Workfare where the site is an authorized Comparable Workfare entity and the worker issues the DCO-261 — exactly the 'workfare_if_registered' pattern. The 80-hour figure is correct for self-initiated general nonprofit volunteering; it is NOT the only available hour rule.
monthly_hours_at_max_allotment: prior implied ~26 (using $291 allotment); corrected to 27 using the FY2026 max 1-person allotment $298 / $11.00.
minimum_wage_usd: now explicitly recorded as 11.00 (prior file referenced $11.00 in prose but did not set a structured field).
remote_stance: prior 'unknown'; normalized to 'silent' (no source addresses it).
unresolved: Exact DCO-261/DCO-0261 fillable form is not publicly posted; field list is inferred from manual descriptions and Appendix A, not from the form itself.
No fixed calendar submission deadline is published; timing is tied to county notification + 10-day determination and the recertification/SAR cycle.
Out-of-state 501(c)(3) eligibility to certify hours is not addressed by any primary source (silent).
Remote/virtual volunteering is not addressed by any primary source (silent).

Whether the new Mandatory E&T (April 2026) ever overrides or supplements the self-initiated 80-hour volunteer lane for a given non-working ABAWD is not fully spelled out in public sources; under 7 CFR 273.24 and AR Section 3620, an ABAWD already meeting 80 hrs/mo (incl. via volunteering) is exempt from mandatory E&T referral, but the interaction is not exhaustively documented.
Secondary claim of '15 rural county exemptions' could not be confirmed against the FNS waiver list and is treated as not a geographic ABAWD waiver.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-verified against the CURRENT Arkansas SNAP Certification Manual Section 3000 (HR1 markup, sections dated 03/01/2026), obtained as a 160-page PDF from the Arkansas Legislature meeting attachment 'DHS DCO SNAP Updates from HR1' (arkleg.state.ar.us), and against the full Complete_SNAP_Manual.pdf (1,057 pages) on humanservices.arkansas.gov containing the full Workfare chapter (3700-3760). Key verbatim findings: (1) Section 3500/3540.3 RTW: 80 hrs/mo, unpaid/volunteer work counts, verified by Volunteer Agreement Form DCO-0261. (2) Section 3730: workfare hours = monthly SNAP allotment / max(state, federal min wage), fractions rounded down — applies to Formal AND Comparable Workfare; manual EXAMPLE $194/$8.50=23 hrs 'rather than 80.' (3) Section 3700: Comparable Workfare providers limited to churches, local government agencies, and other non-profits 'willing to meet the reporting requirements to establish the individual's participation and supervision'; 'may operate in any county.' (4) Section 3751.2: 'Issue the DCO-261 Volunteer Agreement to be completed by the Comparable Workfare entity/individual who will supervise any volunteer activity'; the individual 'may find his or her own placement with one of the allowable entities.' (5) ABAWD age expanded 18-54 -> 18-64 under HR1/OBBBA (ACHI: 'expands the definition of ABAWD ... to include adults ages 18 to 64'); 80-hour requirement unchanged; geographic waivers now restricted to >10% unemployment areas. (6) No AR entry on FNS FY2025-2029 ABAWD waiver list (verified by fetching the FNS waiver index and confirming no 'ar-abawd-response' PDF and zero 'Arkansas' matches). (7) AR min wage 2026 = $11.00 (state ballot Issue 5, no COLA), used in the formula. CLASSIFICATION: workfare_if_registered (HIGH confidence) — the reduced allotment/min-wage formula is real and available but gated on Comparable Workfare provider status; otherwise general nonprofit volunteering counts at the flat 80 hrs/month. This corrects the prior file's federal_80 / tier_1_clean classification.
